objective: Validate all 7 ACs for health-gated explore-mode surface in `grove serve`: mode selection/precedence, startup health gate, explore tool schema, isError paths, provider-loss recovery, byte-identical default mode, and hygiene gates.
key_changes: AC-1 PASS: determine_surface() precedence force_standard > force_explore|config_exists > health_probe; --explore/--standard clap flags in main.rs; mcp.rs stays thin.
AC-2 PASS: health_probe called once pre-loop; healthy → 1 tool (plain object schema, no anyOf/oneOf); unhealthy → 7-tool fallback + 'falling back' stderr note; initialize always succeeds.
AC-3 PASS: call_explore_tool uses real OpenAiCompatClient; missing question → isError:true; all ExploreError variants → isError:true; match exhaustive over ProviderDown|Client.
AC-4 PASS: ExploreError::ProviderDown → isError:true with actionable endpoint/grove-config/restart message; server does not crash or exit.
AC-5 PASS: No config + no --explore flag → Surface::Standard; tool_specs/instructions/call_tool unchanged; all 32 unit tests and 19 integration tests pass.
AC-6 PASS: explore_mode_unhealthy_provider_falls_back_to_standard_surface integration test: port-1 URL → 7 tools + 'falling back' in stderr + initialize OK.
AC-7 PASS: cargo test --release --locked → 178 pass, 0 fail; clippy --all-targets -D warnings → clean; grep fastcontext cli/src/ core/src/ → CLEAN.
Advisory (not blocking): no direct unit test for missing-question isError path or explore_tool_spec() no-anyOf/oneOf shape; exercised transitively.
